description: The Ramashish Koeri gang was a caste-based dacoit band active in the Kaimur plateau region of Bihar, India, during the 1980s. Led by Ramashish Koeri after the death of previous leader Mohan Bind, the gang was responsible for over a hundred murders in the region. It emerged from a context of extreme poverty and feudal repression by Rajput landlords, with members from Scheduled Castes and backward castes. The gang's activities declined after the death of Ramashish Koeri, but it was succeeded by leaders like Kameswar Koeri.
